top_issues.headline: 3 links have no accessible name
top_issues.user_impact: Screen reader users cannot determine where a link goes. Voice control users cannot click links by name. This fails basic WCAG Level A compliance.
top_issues.suggestion: Every link must have a descriptive accessible name. Add visible text between the anchor tags, or add an aria-label if the link contains only an icon. Avoid 'click here' or 'read more' as the sole link text.

top_issues.headline: Page is missing a language declaration
top_issues.user_impact: Screen readers cannot select the correct voice/pronunciation engine. Translation tools cannot detect the language automatically.
top_issues.suggestion: Add a lang attribute to the root <html> element with the appropriate BCP 47 language tag.

top_issues.headline: Page is missing a main landmark
top_issues.user_impact: Keyboard and screen reader users cannot skip to the main content. This is a common navigation barrier for users who visit many pages on your site.
top_issues.suggestion: Wrap the primary page content in a <main> element. Every page should have exactly one <main>.

top_issues.headline: 1 element ensure that the page, or at least one of its frames contains a level-one heading
top_issues.user_impact: This violation affects users relying on assistive technologies.
top_issues.suggestion: Fix this violation by addressing the failing elements. See https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/page-has-heading-one?application=axeAPI for detailed guidance.

quick_wins.headline: Page is missing a language declaration
quick_wins.user_impact: Screen readers cannot select the correct voice/pronunciation engine. Translation tools cannot detect the language automatically.
quick_wins.suggestion: Add a lang attribute to the root <html> element with the appropriate BCP 47 language tag.
